Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: The grid has 3 panels - left (7×4), right-top (3×4), right-bottom (3×4). There are 5 green cells total. I've only clicked green cells in the left panel so far. Let me try clicking a green cell in the right panel.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find the green cells in the right panels
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_cells = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] == 12:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        center_r = (min_r + max_r) // 2
        center_c = (min_c + max_c) // 2
        green_cells.append((min_r, min_c, max_r, max_c, center_r, center_c))
        print(f"Green cell: top-left=({min_r},{min_c}), bottom-right=({max_r},{max_c}), center=({center_r},{center_c})")

# Click on the first green cell in the right panel (should be around row 25, col 43)
# Based on the green_cells list, find one in the right panel
right_green = [g for g in green_cells if g[1] > 30]  # col > 30 is right panel
print(f"\nRight panel green cells: {right_green}")
if right_green:
    g = right_green[0]
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': g[4], 'col': g[5]}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
